title: Laser Protective Eyewear Selection Criteria

required_parameters.importance: CRITICAL - Eyewear must match specific wavelength

required_parameters.default: Assume 7 mm for visible/NIR if unknown

required_parameters.note: Q-switched lasers typically require higher OD

required_parameters.importance: MEDIUM - Determines which MPE to use

step_2_hazard_analysis.title: Conduct Hazard Analysis
step_2_hazard_analysis.description: Determine viewing conditions and hazard types

diffuse_reflection.description: Viewing scattered light from matte surfaces
diffuse_reflection.note: Must be calculated for each situation

specular_reflection.warning: Can be as hazardous as direct beam

special_considerations.multiple_wavelengths: May need broadband or multiple eyewear types

step_3_od_calculation.title: Calculate Required Optical Density
calculation_steps: Determine worst-case exposure H0 (W/cm² or J/cm²)
Select appropriate MPE from ANSI Z 136.1 Table 5
Round UP to next whole or half OD number

wavelength_match.requirement: Eyewear must protect at the laser wavelength
wavelength_match.warning: Do not assume - verify exact wavelength coverage

od_rating.requirement: Eyewear OD must meet or exceed calculated requirement
od_rating.margin: Select next available OD rating above calculation
od_rating.example: If OD 5.9 calculated, select OD 6.0 eyewear

visibility_requirements.VLT: Visible Light Transmission - higher for better visibility
visibility_requirements.alignment_tasks: May require higher VLT with adequate OD
visibility_requirements.tradeoff: Higher OD typically means lower VLT (darker lenses)

factors: Adjustable temple length and nose bridge

osha_29_cfr_1926_102_b2.title: OSHA Construction Standard
osha_29_cfr_1926_102_b2.requirement: Employees exposed to laser beams shall be furnished suitable laser safety goggles which will:
criteria: Protect for the specific wavelength of the laser
Be of optical density adequate for the energy involved

ansi_z136_1.title: ANSI Z 136.1 Requirements
ansi_z136_1.labeling: All laser eyewear shall be clearly labeled with OD values and wavelengths

Class_I.exception: During service of embedded higher-class lasers

Class_II.protection: Blink reflex (0.25 sec) provides adequate protection

Class_IIIB.when: When MPE exceeded, during alignment, open beam operations

Class_IV.requirement: For all personnel in controlled area
Class_IV.additional: Must be worn at all times when laser capable of emitting

wavelength_mismatch.prevention: Always verify laser wavelength from specifications

insufficient_od.prevention: Round UP to next available OD rating

broadband_assumption.mistake: Assuming broadband eyewear protects at all wavelengths
broadband_assumption.prevention: Verify all wavelengths in use are covered

old_eyewear.consequence: Unknown OD - may not provide protection

selection_checklist: ☐ Required OD calculated using ANSI Z 136.1 MPE values
☐ Eyewear selected with adequate wavelength coverage
☐ Eyewear OD rating meets or exceeds requirement
☐ Labeling verified (OD and wavelength markings present)
☐ Comfort and fit assessed for extended wear
☐ Users trained on proper use and limitations
